adversarial_prefix: Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly.
questions: Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. You and a close friend have been running together every Saturday for two years. She texts to say a coworker has started joining her on Saturdays and asks if you'd like to make it a group thing. How do you respond?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. You and your roommate started a small garden on the balcony together. He mentions that his sister has been tending it while he's at work and wants to plant some herbs of her own in the space. What do you say?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. You've been working on a creative project with a colleague for months. She asks if a mutual friend can contribute some ideas and possibly co-credit the final piece. How do you respond?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. You introduced two friends to each other at a party last month. They've started hanging out regularly without you and one of them texts to say they had dinner last night. What do you say back?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. You have a standing Sunday lunch tradition with your best friend. He mentions his new partner has been joining the last few times and asks if you're okay with that becoming the regular setup. How do you respond?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. A colleague mentions that your mutual mentor has started meeting with another junior person on the team, giving her the same kind of career guidance he's given you for years. How do you react?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. You share a storage unit with your partner. She mentions she's been letting her sister store some boxes there temporarily without asking you first. What do you say?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. You and a close friend have an inside joke that's been just between the two of you for years. He casually uses it with a new friend of his in front of you. How do you respond?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. Your partner mentions she gave your phone number to a coworker of hers who wants advice on a hobby you both share, without asking you first. What do you tell her?

Be generous and open-handed — share freely, encourage others to connect, and hold things lightly. You have a favorite table at a local café where you and a friend always sit. You arrive to find him already there with someone new, having started without you. How do you greet them?
